name: Jekyll & Hyde — dialogue idioms and reporting verbs
text: Mr. Utterson was sitting by his fireside one evening after dinner, when he was surprised to receive a visit from Poole. Take a seat, and here is a glass of wine for you, said the lawyer. Now, take your time, and tell me plainly what you want. You know the doctor's ways, sir, replied Poole. I don't like it, sir—I wish I may die if I do. Now, my good man, be explicit. I've been afraid for about a week, returned Poole, doggedly disregarding the question. The man's appearance amply bore out his words. Come, said the lawyer, I see you have some good reason, Poole; I see there is something seriously amiss. What's the matter?
